subject: Aerial view of Pincehely village showing the Church of the Visitation, 2007
context_for_lesson: Aerial photograph of Pincehely showing the village layout and the Church of the Visitation (a single-nave Roman Catholic church built in 1762). Provides geographic / scale context for students to understand the small-village setting where Judy was hidden in 1944.
author: Civertan
license_summary: CC BY-SA 3.0 — free to use, share, and adapt with attribution; derivative works must use the same license
lesson_use_intent: Geographic context — aerial shot helps Grade 6 students see how small Pincehely is compared to Budapest. Pair on a journey-map page or 'Pincehely village' page.
resolution_pixels: moderate (~43KB — note: this is a smaller file; if higher resolution is needed, an alternative aerial photo from Wikimedia Commons may exist)
concerns: Smaller file size (~43KB). At Grade 6 textbook print size (1/3 page) this should still display acceptably, but check rendering before final ship. License is explicit and clean.
